setup: Prospect hiring spike is entirely in customer support ops, but the buyer targeted is VP Engineering.
expected_failure: System frames the account as an engineering outsourcing opportunity.

setup: Prospect is a regulated bank hiring only compliance analysts while SignalForge matches a technical ICP.
expected_failure: Message claims engineering capacity pressure with no technical evidence.

setup: Prospect is hiring an internal data science team, but the workflow selects generic staff augmentation positioning.
expected_failure: The pitch mismatches the actual buying center and needed capability.

setup: Funding is recent, but the company only moved from 2 to 3 open roles in sixty days.
expected_failure: System says the company is scaling aggressively.

setup: A layoff event exists without rebound hiring, but the pitch treats it as outsourcing urgency.
expected_failure: Message sounds predatory and overstates need.

setup: Funding event is older than 180 days and velocity is flat.
expected_failure: Email still claims fresh post-round hiring urgency.

setup: Prospect is building a security engineering function with clear indications they want permanent leadership hires.
expected_failure: System defaults to short-term outsourcing language instead of capability partnership language.

setup: The prospect has strong internal AI maturity and likely needs niche specialists, not generic augmentation.
expected_failure: Message reads like commodity offshore staffing.

setup: Prospect just hired a CTO and public messaging emphasizes long-term internal capability building.
expected_failure: System leads with headcount rental instead of team design support.

setup: Medium-confidence signals are present but the prompt generates enthusiastic SDR language.
expected_failure: Tone becomes salesy instead of informed and calm.

setup: A prospect had layoffs and leadership change in the same quarter.
expected_failure: Message sounds opportunistic rather than respectful of uncertainty.

setup: Low-confidence account still receives a polished benchmark pitch rather than a calibration question.
expected_failure: The note hides uncertainty instead of surfacing it.

setup: A reply from one prospect thread updates state keyed only by company domain shared across subsidiaries.
expected_failure: SMS eligibility and CRM notes leak across threads.

setup: Two contacts at the same company receive outreach and one reply unlocks SMS for both.
expected_failure: System sends an SMS to a contact who never replied to email.

setup: Voice queue reads stale state from a previous prospect run with the same phone number fixture.
expected_failure: Voice escalation is queued for the wrong lead.

setup: Low-confidence prospects still go through full LLM generation and strict-regeneration loops.
expected_failure: System spends tokens on threads that should have taken deterministic fallback immediately.

setup: Identical prompts are regenerated because caching keys ignore confidence behavior changes.
expected_failure: Repeated runs miss cache and inflate cost.

setup: Provider failures still trigger downstream CRM and Langfuse work for a send that never happened.
expected_failure: Operational cost rises and logs become noisy.

setup: CRM sync runs before state store save and records the wrong lifecycle stage.
expected_failure: HubSpot shows stale stage while the state machine moves ahead.

setup: Calendar webhook marks a booking complete but the conversation service never updates allowed next channels.
expected_failure: Follow-up outreach continues after a meeting is booked.

setup: Inbound email reply is accepted by the provider webhook but not routed to state update logic.
expected_failure: SMS warm lead gate remains closed even though the buyer replied.

setup: Email offers a booking link without clarifying timezone and the buyer is in CET while the SDR is in US Eastern.
expected_failure: Meeting slots appear off by one hour around DST transitions.

setup: SMS follow-up references 'tomorrow morning' to an East Africa lead while the system runs in UTC.
expected_failure: Relative-time messaging is wrong for the recipient.

setup: Calendar webhook payload uses local time with no zone offset from a regional workspace.
expected_failure: CRM records the wrong meeting time.

setup: Crunchbase record is missing for a manually entered prospect.
expected_failure: Run crashes instead of producing a sparse, honest brief.

setup: Playwright is unavailable locally and the job scraper does not surface the fallback reason.
expected_failure: Engineers think live scraping happened when only fixture parsing ran.

setup: Leadership change fixture exists but uses a malformed date.
expected_failure: Leadership signal silently disappears and lowers segment quality.

setup: Only two peers have a public AI practice, but the summary says 'most competitors'.
expected_failure: Benchmark language overstates the sector pattern.

setup: Sparse sector with only three peers still yields a confident benchmark rank.
expected_failure: System overstates peer confidence instead of flagging sparse evidence.

setup: Named competitor tool or role is public, but the prospect has no direct comparable signal.
expected_failure: Message implies the prospect is behind instead of saying no public signal was observed.

setup: Overall confidence lands at 0.53 with one stale funding signal and one modest hiring signal.
expected_failure: System still sends a directional benchmark email instead of a calibration question.

setup: Competitor gap confidence is low, but the email still names a clear maturity deficit.
expected_failure: Confidence is treated as presentation metadata instead of a hard control surface.

setup: Prospect replies 'maybe' and the follow-up flow immediately offers SMS plus booking language.
expected_failure: Workflow treats ambiguous intent like qualified intent.

setup: Confidence is low but the LLM path still runs before deterministic fallback.
expected_failure: Weak evidence gets more fluent instead of more constrained.

setup: Message mentions offshore speed or cost savings to a Tenacious target who values embedded team quality.
expected_failure: Prospect reads Tenacious as a low-cost outsourcing shop.

setup: A recently hired CTO receives a message implying the team is behind peers without acknowledging uncertainty.
expected_failure: The note triggers defensiveness instead of curiosity.
